You said:

<!-- MySQL BACKEND SETUP ───────────────────────────────────────── CREATE DATABASE arshit_internships; USE arshit_internships; CREATE TABLE applications ( id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, role VARCHAR(100), company VARCHAR(100), first_name VARCHAR(60), last_name VARCHAR(60), email VARCHAR(120), phone VARCHAR(20), city VARCHAR(60), qualification VARCHAR(60), year_of_study VARCHAR(40), skills TEXT, motivation TEXT, portfolio VARCHAR(255), applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ); submit_application.php: <?php header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *"); header("Content-Type: application/json"); $conn = new mysqli("localhost","root","YOUR_PASSWORD","arshit_internships"); $data = json_decode(file_get_contents("php://input"), true); $stmt = $conn->prepare("INSERT INTO applications (role,company,first_name,last_name,email,phone,city,qualification,year_of_study,skills,motivation,portfolio) VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)"); $stmt->bind_param("ssssssssssss", $data['role'],$data['company'],$data['first_name'],$data['last_name'], $data['email'],$data['phone'],$data['city'],$data['qualification'], $data['year_of_study'],$data['skills'],$data['motivation'],$data['portfolio']); echo json_encode(["success"=>$stmt->execute()]); $conn->close(); ?> ───────────────────────────────────────── --> The full SQL + PHP backend code is in the HTML comments. To go live: Run the CREATE TABLE SQL in your MySQL database Create submit_application.php on your server (code is in the comments) Update the API_URL variable in the JS to point to your PHP file what to do give step by step

ChatGPT said:

Alright Advay’s got you 😎
You already have the SQL + PHP backend code. Now follow these steps carefully to make your internship form fully working with MySQL + PHP 🚀

🛠 STEP 1 — Install XAMPP

Download and install:

XAMPP Official Website

During installation:

  • Select:

    • Apache ✅

    • MySQL ✅

    • PHP ✅

After install:

  • Open XAMPP Control Panel

  • Start:

    • Apache 🟢

    • MySQL 🟢

📁 STEP 2 — Open htdocs Folder

Go to:

C:\xampp\htdocs\

Create a new folder:

arshit-internships

Inside it place:

  • your HTML file

  • submit_application.php

Example:

htdocs/
 └── arshit-internships/
      ├── index.html
      └── submit_application.php

🗄 STEP 3 — Create Database

Open browser:

http://localhost/phpmyadmin

Now:

1. Click “New”

Create database:

SQL
arshit_internships

Click Create

📄 STEP 4 — Create Table

Click your database → SQL tab

Paste this:

SQL
CREATE TABLE applications (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    role VARCHAR(100),
    company VARCHAR(100),
    first_name VARCHAR(60),
    last_name VARCHAR(60),
    email VARCHAR(120),
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    city VARCHAR(60),
    qualification VARCHAR(60),
    year_of_study VARCHAR(40),
    skills TEXT,
    motivation TEXT,
    portfolio VARCHAR(255),
    applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

Click ✅ GO

Done 🎉

🧾 STEP 5 — Create submit_application.php

Inside:

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\

Create:

submit_application.php

Paste this FULL code:

PHP
<?php

header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *");
header("Content-Type: application/json");

$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

if ($conn->connect_error) {
    die(json_encode([
        "success" => false,
        "message" => "Database connection failed"
    ]));
}

$data = json_decode(file_get_contents("php://input"), true);

$stmt = $conn->prepare("
INSERT INTO applications
(role, company, first_name, last_name, email, phone, city,
qualification, year_of_study, skills, motivation, portfolio)

VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
");

$stmt->bind_param(
    "ssssssssssss",
    $data['role'],
    $data['company'],
    $data['first_name'],
    $data['last_name'],
    $data['email'],
    $data['phone'],
    $data['city'],
    $data['qualification'],
    $data['year_of_study'],
    $data['skills'],
    $data['motivation'],
    $data['portfolio']
);

if ($stmt->execute()) {
    echo json_encode([
        "success" => true,
        "message" => "Application submitted successfully"
    ]);
} else {
    echo json_encode([
        "success" => false,
        "message" => "Error submitting application"
    ]);
}

$conn->close();

?>

🌐 STEP 6 — Update API_URL in JavaScript

In your HTML file find:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "";

Replace with:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php";

▶ STEP 7 — Run Website

Open browser:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/

OR

http://localhost/arshit-internships/index.html

🧪 STEP 8 — Test Form

Fill form → Click Submit

If everything is correct:
✅ Data saves in MySQL

Check in phpMyAdmin:

  • Open database

  • applications table

  • Browse tab

You’ll see submitted applications 🎉

❌ COMMON ERRORS FIX

Apache not starting

Usually port issue.

Fix:

  • Close Skype/Teams

  • Or change Apache port in XAMPP

MySQL not starting

Delete old MySQL processes from Task Manager.

“Failed to fetch”

Means:

  • Apache not running

  • Wrong API_URL

  • PHP file not in correct folder

📦 FINAL FOLDER STRUCTURE

arshit-internships/
│
├── index.html
├── style.css
├── script.js
└── submit_application.php

🚀 AFTER EVERYTHING WORKS

You can later host it online using:

  • InfinityFree

  • 000webhost

  • Hostinger

Then update:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "https://yourdomain.com/submit_application.php";

and it becomes LIVE 🌍🔥


You said:

bro i have mysqlworkbench and mysql server

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 😎
Then you do NOT need XAMPP MySQL because you already have:

  • ✅ MySQL Workbench

  • ✅ MySQL Server

You only need:

  • Apache + PHP to run the PHP file

Easiest way 👉 install only XAMPP for Apache/PHP OR use VS Code PHP server.

✅ BEST METHOD FOR YOU (Easy)

STEP 1 — Install XAMPP

Download:

XAMPP Download

During installation:
❌ Uncheck MySQL
✅ Keep:

  • Apache

  • PHP

Because you already have MySQL Server installed.

✅ STEP 2 — Start Apache Only

Open XAMPP Control Panel.

Start ONLY:

  • Apache 🟢

Do NOT start MySQL from XAMPP.

✅ STEP 3 — Create Database in MySQL Workbench

Open MySQL Workbench

Click SQL tab and run:

SQL
CREATE DATABASE arshit_internships;

USE arshit_internships;

CREATE TABLE applications (
    id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
    role VARCHAR(100),
    company VARCHAR(100),
    first_name VARCHAR(60),
    last_name VARCHAR(60),
    email VARCHAR(120),
    phone VARCHAR(20),
    city VARCHAR(60),
    qualification VARCHAR(60),
    year_of_study VARCHAR(40),
    skills TEXT,
    motivation TEXT,
    portfolio VARCHAR(255),
    applied_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

Click ⚡ Execute.

Done 🎉

✅ STEP 4 — Put Project in htdocs

Go to:

C:\xampp\htdocs\

Create folder:

arshit-internships

Put:

  • index.html

  • submit_application.php

inside it.

✅ STEP 5 — Update PHP Password

IMPORTANT ⚠

Open:

submit_application.php

Find:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

Replace with YOUR MySQL password.

Example:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "mypassword123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

If your MySQL root password is empty:
leave ""

✅ STEP 6 — Update API URL

In JS:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php";

✅ STEP 7 — Run Website

Open browser:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/

✅ STEP 8 — Test Submission

Submit form.

Then check in MySQL Workbench:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

If rows appear 🔥
YOUR BACKEND IS WORKING.

🧠 IMPORTANT

Why VS Code Live Server won’t work alone?

Because:

  • HTML works in browser

  • BUT PHP needs Apache server

That’s why we use:

  • Apache (XAMPP)

  • PHP

  • MySQL Server

🚀 ALTERNATIVE (WITHOUT XAMPP)

You can also use PHP built-in server:

Open terminal in project folder:

Bash
php -S localhost:8000

Then open:

http://localhost:8000

But Apache + XAMPP is easier for beginners 👍

ChatGPT said:

No worries 😄 I’ll show you exactly step-by-step.

📁 STEP 4 — Put Project in htdocs

🔹 1. Open File Explorer

Press:

Windows + E

This opens File Explorer.

🔹 2. Open XAMPP Folder

On left side click:

  • This PC

Then open:

Local Disk (C:)

Now open:

xampp

Inside xampp open:

htdocs

Final path:

C:\xampp\htdocs

🔹 3. Create New Folder

Inside htdocs

👉 Right click empty space

👉 Click:

  • New

  • Folder

Name it:

arshit-internships

Press Enter ✅

🔹 4. Open Your Project Folder

Now open the folder where your:

  • index.html

  • CSS

  • JS files

are saved.

Example:

  • Desktop

  • Downloads

  • VS Code project folder

🔹 5. Copy Files

Select:

  • index.html

  • style.css

  • script.js

  • images

  • other files

Right click → Copy

OR

Ctrl + C

🔹 6. Paste Into htdocs Folder

Go back to:

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships

Right click → Paste

OR

Ctrl + V

🔹 7. Create submit_application.php

Inside arshit-internships folder:

👉 Right click empty area
👉 New → Text Document

Rename:

submit_application.php

⚠ VERY IMPORTANT:
Remove .txt

NOT:

submit_application.php.txt

Correct:

submit_application.php

🔹 8. Paste PHP Code

Right click submit_application.php

Open with:

  • Notepad
    OR

  • Visual Studio Code

Paste the PHP code I gave earlier.

Save:

Ctrl + S

🔹 9. Final Folder Should Look Like This

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\
│
├── index.html
├── style.css
├── script.js
├── submit_application.php
└── images

🔹 10. Run Website

Open browser and type:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/

🔥 Your website should open.

in vs code having my code step 6 i did after that explain what to do7. Create submit_application.php Inside arshit-internships folder: 👉 Right click empty area 👉 New → Text Document Rename: submit_application.php ⚠ VERY IMPORTANT: Remove .txt NOT: submit_application.php.txt Correct: submit_application.php 🔹 8. Paste PHP Code Right click submit_application.php Open with: Notepad OR Visual Studio Code Paste the PHP code I gave earlier. Save: Ctrl + S 🔹 9. Final Folder Should Look Like This C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\ │ ├── index.html ├── style.css ├── script.js ├── submit_application.php └── images 🔹 10. Run Website Open browser and type: http://localhost/arshit-internships/ 🔥 Your website should open.

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 😎 You already have your code in Visual Studio Code.
Now do ONLY these next steps carefully 👇

✅ STEP 7 — Create submit_application.php IN VS CODE

🔹 1. Open Your Project Folder in VS Code

Left side Explorer panel should show:

index.html
style.css
script.js

and other files.

🔹 2. Create New PHP File

In left Explorer panel:

👉 Right click your project folder
👉 Click:

New File

Type EXACTLY:

submit_application.php

Press Enter ✅

DONE 🎉

✅ STEP 8 — Paste PHP Code

Open the new file:

submit_application.php

Paste this FULL code 👇

PHP
<?php

header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *");
header("Content-Type: application/json");

$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

if ($conn->connect_error) {
    die(json_encode([
        "success" => false,
        "message" => "Database connection failed"
    ]));
}

$data = json_decode(file_get_contents("php://input"), true);

$stmt = $conn->prepare("
INSERT INTO applications
(role, company, first_name, last_name, email, phone, city,
qualification, year_of_study, skills, motivation, portfolio)

VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
");

$stmt->bind_param(
    "ssssssssssss",
    $data['role'],
    $data['company'],
    $data['first_name'],
    $data['last_name'],
    $data['email'],
    $data['phone'],
    $data['city'],
    $data['qualification'],
    $data['year_of_study'],
    $data['skills'],
    $data['motivation'],
    $data['portfolio']
);

if ($stmt->execute()) {
    echo json_encode([
        "success" => true,
        "message" => "Application submitted successfully"
    ]);
} else {
    echo json_encode([
        "success" => false,
        "message" => "Error submitting application"
    ]);
}

$conn->close();

?>

✅ STEP 9 — SAVE FILE

Press:

Ctrl + S

VERY IMPORTANT ⚠

✅ STEP 10 — Move Entire Project Folder to htdocs

Now your VS Code project folder contains:

index.html
style.css
script.js
submit_application.php

Good 🔥

Now:

Open File Explorer

Find your project folder.

Example:

Desktop/arshit-internships

OR wherever your project is saved.

✅ STEP 11 — COPY PROJECT FOLDER

Right click your whole project folder:

arshit-internships

Click:

Copy

✅ STEP 12 — PASTE INTO XAMPP htdocs

Go to:

C:\xampp\htdocs\

Paste folder there.

FINAL should become:

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships

✅ STEP 13 — START APACHE

Open XAMPP Control Panel.

Click:

Start

beside Apache.

Apache should become GREEN 🟢

✅ STEP 14 — OPEN WEBSITE

Open browser.

Type:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/

🔥 Website opens.

✅ STEP 15 — TEST DATABASE CONNECTION

Fill your internship form.

Click submit.

Then open MySQL Workbench and run:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

If data appears 🎉
YOUR FULL STACK WEBSITE IS LIVE LOCALLY 🚀🔥


You said:

i can see the table but if we submit in anm not getting data

ChatGPT said:

Good 😎
That means:

  • ✅ Database created

  • ✅ Table created

  • ✅ Website opening

Now only the FORM → PHP connection has issue.

We’ll fix it 🔥

✅ STEP 1 — CHECK API URL

Open your JS file or HTML.

Find:

JavaScript
const API_URL =

Make sure it is EXACTLY:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php";

Save:

Ctrl + S

✅ STEP 2 — TEST PHP FILE DIRECTLY

Open browser and type:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php

What do you see?

You should see:

  • blank page
    OR

  • small JSON error

❌ If you see:

  • 404

  • file not found

  • apache error

then PHP file location is wrong.

✅ STEP 3 — CHECK MYSQL PASSWORD

Open:

submit_application.php

Find:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

If your MySQL has password:

Example:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "YOURPASSWORD",
    "arshit_internships"
);

Save file.

✅ STEP 4 — ENABLE CONSOLE ERRORS

Open website.

Press:

F12

Go to:

Console

Now submit form.

Tell me:

  • what RED error appears.

Example:

  • Failed to fetch

  • 500 error

  • CORS error

  • API_URL undefined

That error will tell exact problem 🔥

✅ STEP 5 — MOST COMMON ISSUE

Usually your fetch code is wrong.

Use THIS EXACT submit code 👇

Replace your current fetch code with:

JavaScript
fetch(API_URL, {
    method: "POST",
    headers: {
        "Content-Type": "application/json"
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
        role,
        company,
        first_name,
        last_name,
        email,
        phone,
        city,
        qualification,
        year_of_study,
        skills,
        motivation,
        portfolio
    })
})
.then(res => res.json())
.then(data => {
    console.log(data);

    if(data.success){
        alert("Application Submitted Successfully!");
    } else {
        alert("Submission Failed");
    }
})
.catch(err => {
    console.error(err);
});

✅ STEP 6 — IMPORTANT

If form uses:

HTML
<form>

Change to:

HTML
<form id="applicationForm">

AND in JS:

JavaScript
document.getElementById("applicationForm")

Otherwise submit may refresh page before sending data.

✅ STEP 7 — PREVENT PAGE REFRESH

Inside submit event MUST have:

JavaScript
e.preventDefault();

Example:

JavaScript
form.addEventListener("submit", function(e){
    e.preventDefault();

    // fetch code
});

🚀 MOST LIKELY FIX

99% chance:

  • wrong API_URL
    OR

  • missing e.preventDefault()
    OR

  • PHP password wrong

Send me:

  1. your fetch code

  2. console red error screenshot/message

and I’ll fix it immediately 😎


You said:

bro i forgot password of mysqlworkbench . it has localhost:3306

ChatGPT said:

Got it 😄
localhost:3306 is just the MySQL server address and port — not the password.

If you forgot the MySQL root password, easiest fix is to RESET it 🔥

✅ METHOD 1 — Try Empty Password First

In your PHP file:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

Notice:

""

means NO password.

Save → test form.

Many MySQL installations use empty password by default.

❌ If Still Not Working → Reset Password

✅ STEP 1 — Stop MySQL Service

Press:

Windows + R

Type:

services.msc

Press Enter.

Find:

MySQL80

(or MySQL)

Right click → Stop

✅ STEP 2 — Open CMD as Administrator

Search:

cmd

Right click:

Run as administrator

✅ STEP 3 — Go to MySQL bin Folder

Usually:

cd "C:\Program Files\MySQL\MySQL Server 8.0\bin"

Press Enter.

✅ STEP 4 — Start MySQL Without Password

Run:

Bash
mysqld --skip-grant-tables

⚠ KEEP THIS WINDOW OPEN.

✅ STEP 5 — Open NEW CMD Window

Again open another CMD as administrator.

Run:

Bash
mysql -u root

You should enter MySQL.

✅ STEP 6 — Reset Password

Run these one by one:

SQL
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;
SQL
ALTER USER 'root'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED BY 'newpassword123';

Example password:

newpassword123

You can set your own.

✅ STEP 7 — Close Everything

Close CMD windows.

Go back to:

services.msc

Start:

MySQL80

✅ STEP 8 — Test in MySQL Workbench

Open MySQL Workbench

Password:

newpassword123

✅ STEP 9 — UPDATE PHP FILE

Now change PHP:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "newpassword123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

Save ✅

🚀 TEST AGAIN

Submit form again.

Then run:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

You should finally see data 🎉🔥

"); $stmt->bind_param( "ssssssssssss", $data['role'], $data['company'], $data['first_name'], $data['last_name'], $data['email'], $data['phone'], $data['city'], $data['qualification'], $data['year_of_study'], $data['skills'], $data['motivation'], $data['portfolio'] ); if ($stmt->execute()) { echo json_encode([ "success" => true, "message" => "Application submitted successfully" ]); } else { echo json_encode([ "success" => false, "message" => "Error submitting application" ]); } $conn->close(); ?> should i simply run this

Yes 😄🔥
That PHP code is correct.

You need to run it through Apache/XAMPP.

🔹 1. Save File

File name MUST be:

submit_application.php

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\

✅ 2. Start Apache

Open XAMPP Control Panel.

Click:

Start

beside Apache.

Apache should become GREEN 🟢

✅ 3. Test PHP File

Open browser.

Type:

✅ What Should Happen?

You may see:

  • blank page ✅
    OR

  • JSON error ✅

Both are okay because no form data was sent yet.

404 error

Means:

  • file not in correct folder

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\

Means:

  • MySQL password wrong
    OR

http://localhost/arshit-internships/

then problem is in JavaScript fetch code, not PHP.

You said:

Not Found The requested URL was not found on this server. Apache/2.4.58 (Win64) OpenSSL/3.1.3 PHP/8.2.12 Server at localhost Port 80

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 😄🔥
This means Apache is working ✅
BUT your PHP file is NOT in the correct folder.

Let’s fix it in 2 minutes.

✅ STEP 1 — Find Your Project Folder

Open Visual Studio Code

Left side check your project name.

Example:

arshit-internships

✅ STEP 2 — Open Folder Location

In VS Code:

👉 Right click project folder
👉 Click:

Reveal in File Explorer

Now your real folder opens.

✅ STEP 3 — CHECK FILES

Inside folder you MUST see:

index.html
submit_application.php

If PHP file missing:
create it again.

✅ STEP 4 — MOVE PROJECT TO htdocs

Now VERY IMPORTANT ⚠

Cut or copy the ENTIRE folder:

Example:

arshit-internships

Paste into:

C:\xampp\htdocs\

Final path MUST become:

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\

✅ STEP 5 — VERIFY PHP FILE PATH

Inside:

C:\xampp\htdocs\arshit-internships\

You MUST see:

submit_application.php

✅ STEP 6 — TEST AGAIN

Now open browser:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php

🔥 It should open now.

❌ IF STILL NOT FOUND

Then your folder name is different.

Example:
Maybe actual folder is:

Arshit-Internships-main

Then URL becomes:

http://localhost/Arshit-Internships-main/submit_application.php

⚠ URL folder name MUST EXACTLY match actual folder name.

✅ QUICK CHECK

Open:

C:\xampp\htdocs\

Tell me EXACT folder name you see there 😄


You said:

my project name is website

ChatGPT said:

Aha 😄🔥 that’s the issue.

Your folder name is:

website

So your URL should NOT be:

http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php

Use THIS instead ✅

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

✅ ALSO UPDATE API_URL

In your JS code change:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "http://localhost/arshit-internships/submit_application.php";

TO:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "http://localhost/website/submit_application.php";

Save:

Ctrl + S

✅ TEST NOW

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

If it opens 🎉
Then backend working.

✅ NEXT

Now open:

http://localhost/website/

Fill form → submit.

Then check in MySQL Workbench:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

🔥 You should finally see the submitted data.


You said:

bro shouls i create new folder in name of wensiite in htdocs and paste all in this folder website

ChatGPT said:

YESSS 😄🔥
Exactly bro.

Because your project name is website, Apache looks for:

C:\xampp\htdocs\website\

So do this 👇

✅ STEP-BY-STEP

🔹 1. Open File Explorer

Press:

Windows + E

🔹 2. Go to htdocs

Open:

C:\xampp\htdocs\

🔹 3. Create Folder

Right click empty space → New → Folder

Name it EXACTLY:

website

🔹 4. Open Your Current Project Folder

Where your VS Code files are saved.

You should see:

index.html
style.css
script.js
submit_application.php

🔹 5. Copy ALL Files

Select everything.

Press:

Ctrl + C

🔹 6. Paste Into htdocs/website

Open:

C:\xampp\htdocs\website\

Paste files:

Ctrl + V

✅ FINAL STRUCTURE

Must look EXACTLY like:

C:\xampp\htdocs\website\
│
├── index.html
├── style.css
├── script.js
├── submit_application.php
└── images

✅ STEP 7 — START APACHE

Open XAMPP.

Start Apache 🟢

✅ STEP 8 — TEST PHP

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

If page opens 🎉 SUCCESS.

✅ STEP 9 — OPEN WEBSITE

http://localhost/website/

✅ STEP 10 — TEST FORM

Submit form.

Then check in MySQL Workbench:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

🔥 Your data should appear now 😎


You said:

<?php header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *"); header("Content-Type: application/json"); $conn = new mysqli( "localhost", "root", "", "arshit_internships" ); if ($conn->connect_error) { die(json_encode([ "success" => false, "message" => "Database connection failed" ])); } $data = json_decode(file_get_contents("php://input"), true); $stmt = $conn->prepare(" INSERT INTO applications (role, company, first_name, last_name, email, phone, city, qualification, year_of_study, skills, motivation, portfolio) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?) "); $stmt->bind_param( "ssssssssssss", $data['role'], $data['company'], $data['first_name'], $data['last_name'], $data['email'], $data['phone'], $data['city'], $data['qualification'], $data['year_of_study'], $data['skills'], $data['motivation'], $data['portfolio'] ); if ($stmt->execute()) { echo json_encode([ "success" => true, "message" => "Application submitted successfully" ]); } else { echo json_encode([ "success" => false, "message" => "Error submitting application" ]); } $conn->close(); ?> should i change anything

ChatGPT said:

Your PHP code looks correct 👍🔥

You only need to change ONE thing IF your MySQL has a password.

Right now you have:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

The "" means:
👉 no MySQL password.

✅ IF YOUR MYSQL PASSWORD IS EMPTY

Then DON'T change anything 😄

Keep it exactly like this.

✅ IF YOU HAVE MYSQL PASSWORD

Change:

PHP
""

to your password.

Example:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "mypassword123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

✅ NOW DO THESE 3 THINGS

1️⃣ Save file

Press:

Ctrl + S

2️⃣ Test PHP URL

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

If page opens:
✅ PHP working.

3️⃣ MOST IMPORTANT — UPDATE JS API_URL

Your form JS MUST have:

JavaScript
const API_URL = "http://localhost/website/submit_application.php";

⚠ NOT arshit-internships anymore.

Save again.

✅ FINAL TEST

Open:

http://localhost/website/

Submit form.

Then check in MySQL Workbench:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

🔥 Data should appear now.


You said:

<br /> <b>Fatal error</b>: Uncaught mysqli_sql_exception: Access denied for user 'root'@'localhost' (using password: NO) in C:\xampp\htdocs\website\submit_application.php:6 Stack trace: #0 C:\xampp\htdocs\website\submit_application.php(6): mysqli-&gt;__construct('localhost', 'root', Object(SensitiveParameterValue), 'arshit_internsh...') #1 {main} thrown in <b>C:\xampp\htdocs\website\submit_application.php</b> on line <b>6</b><br />

ChatGPT said:

Perfect 😄🔥
Now we found the REAL issue.

This error means:

Access denied for user 'root'@'localhost'
(using password: NO)

👉 Your MySQL HAS a password
👉 But your PHP is trying WITHOUT password.

✅ FIX

Open:

submit_application.php

Find this:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

✅ Replace With Your MySQL Password

Example:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "mypassword123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

⚠ Replace:

mypassword123

with YOUR actual MySQL password.

✅ SAVE FILE

Press:

Ctrl + S

✅ TEST AGAIN

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

If password correct:
✅ no fatal error anymore.

❓ DON’T KNOW PASSWORD?

Then easiest solution:

Open MySQL Workbench

When it connects:
👉 click connection
👉 Edit Connection

You may see:

  • Store in Vault

  • Show password

OR reset password there.

✅ AFTER FIXING PASSWORD

Test form again.

Then run:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

🔥 Your submitted data will finally appear.


You said:

step by step

ChatGPT said:

Alright 😄🔥
We’ll fix the MySQL password issue step-by-step very slowly.

✅ STEP 1 — Open MySQL Workbench

Open MySQL Workbench

You’ll see your connection like:

Local instance MySQL80

✅ STEP 2 — Click the Connection

Click your MySQL connection.

If it opens database successfully 🎉
that means password is already saved inside Workbench.

✅ STEP 3 — Open Connection Settings

On home screen:

👉 Right click your connection

Example:

Local instance MySQL80

Click:

Edit Connection

✅ STEP 4 — Find Password

Inside connection settings:

Look for:

Password : Store in Vault...

Click:

Store in Vault...

✅ STEP 5 — Show Password

It may ask Windows password.

Then you can:

  • see password
    OR

  • change password

✅ STEP 6 — Copy Password

Example password:

root123

Copy it.

✅ STEP 7 — Open submit_application.php

Open Visual Studio Code

Open:

submit_application.php

Find this:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

✅ STEP 8 — Paste Password

Change to:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "root123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

⚠ Replace:

root123

with YOUR password.

✅ STEP 9 — Save File

Press:

Ctrl + S

✅ STEP 10 — TEST PHP FILE

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

✅ If password correct:
No red fatal error.

Maybe blank page.
That is GOOD 🎉

✅ STEP 11 — TEST WEBSITE

Open:

http://localhost/website/

Fill form.

Click submit.

✅ STEP 12 — CHECK DATABASE

Open MySQL Workbench

Run:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

🔥 Submitted data should appear.


You said:

not showing

ChatGPT said:

No worries 😄 We’ll get the password another way.

✅ EASY METHOD — RESET PASSWORD TO SOMETHING YOU KNOW

We’ll make password:

root123

✅ STEP 1 — Stop MySQL

Press:

Windows + R

Type:

services.msc

Press Enter.

Find:

MySQL80

Right click → Stop

✅ STEP 2 — Open CMD as Admin

Search:

cmd

Right click:

Run as administrator

✅ STEP 3 — Open MySQL bin Folder

Copy paste this:

Bash
cd "C:\Program Files\MySQL\MySQL Server 8.0\bin"

Press Enter.

✅ STEP 4 — Start MySQL Without Password

Paste:

Bash
mysqld --skip-grant-tables

Press Enter.

⚠ KEEP THIS WINDOW OPEN.

✅ STEP 5 — Open SECOND CMD

Again:

  • search cmd

  • Run as administrator

✅ STEP 6 — Login Without Password

Paste:

Bash
mysql -u root

Press Enter.

You should enter MySQL.

✅ STEP 7 — RESET PASSWORD

Paste ONE BY ONE:

SQL
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;

Then:

SQL
ALTER USER 'root'@'localhost' IDENTIFIED BY 'root123';

Press Enter after each.

✅ STEP 8 — CLOSE EVERYTHING

Close both CMD windows.

✅ STEP 9 — Start MySQL Again

Open:

services.msc

Find:

MySQL80

Right click → Start

✅ STEP 10 — UPDATE PHP FILE

Open:

submit_application.php

Replace with:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "root123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

Save:

Ctrl + S

✅ STEP 11 — TEST

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

No fatal error now 🎉

✅ STEP 12 — SUBMIT FORM

Open:

http://localhost/website/

Submit form.

Then run:

SQL
SELECT * FROM applications;

🔥 DONEEEE.

submit_application.php

make sure this matches your new password:

PHP

    "localhost",

    "arshit_internships"
);

YOUR_PASSWORD

with the password you set.

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

http://localhost/website/

SELECT * FROM applications;

Brooo 😄🔥
The error is still saying:

(using password: NO)

That means your PHP file STILL has:

PHP
""

(empty password)

OR the file was not saved.

✅ FIX NOW

Open Visual Studio Code

Open:

submit_application.php

✅ FIND THIS

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "",
    "arshit_internships"
);

✅ CHANGE TO THIS

Example:

PHP
$conn = new mysqli(
    "localhost",
    "root",
    "root123",
    "arshit_internships"
);

⚠ Replace:

root123

with YOUR actual password.

✅ VERY IMPORTANT — SAVE FILE

Press:

Ctrl + S

✅ CHECK IF SAVED

After saving, the line should STILL show:

PHP
"root123"

NOT:

PHP
""

✅ TEST AGAIN

Open browser:

http://localhost/website/submit_application.php

🔥 Fatal error should disappear now.

⚠ IF STILL SAME ERROR

Then you are editing WRONG FILE.

Check the path at top of VS Code.

It MUST be:

C:\xampp\htdocs\website\submit_application.php

NOT another folder copy.

That’s the most common issue 😄
